name: Tsourekia- Greek Easter Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve yeast in the warm water and add enough flour to make a thick batter. Cover and let rise until doubled.
This is called the "sponge".
2. In a large bowl, beat butter and sugar very light and fluffy for about 20 minutes.
3. Beat in the eggs (except for 3 yolks). Alternate adding flour, yoghurt, and milk to the egg/sugar mixture and stir in the yeast mixture.
4. Add enough flour to make a non-sticky dough. Knead until smooth and elastic.
5. Cover dough in a large greased bowl and let rise until doubled.
6. Punch down the dough and let it rise again until doubled.
7. Shape as desired in pans or braided rings. Let rise until doubled and brush loaves with the reserved egg yolks.
8. Sprinkle with sesame seeds. Traditionally, red colored hard cooked eggs may be pressed into the braided ring before the 3rd rising.
9. Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F (175°C) until golden, about 30 minutes.
10. Loaves are light, sweet, and delicious. Note: The recipe may be easily halved.
